name: Vetter Veterinary Software API
what_it_does: Vetter Veterinary Software REST API for cloud-based veterinary practice information management (PIMS) targeting small and mobile veterinary practices. Enables AI agents to manage patient and client records for veterinary record automation, handle appointment scheduling and calendar management for practice booking, access electronic medical records and SOAP note generation for clinical documentation, retrieve inventory and medication management for veterinary pharmacy, manage invoicing and payment processing for practice revenue cycle, handle client communication and automated reminder workflows, access diagnostic lab integration for laboratory result management, retrieve reporting and practice analytics for business performance, manage multi-doctor and mobile practice scheduling for distributed veterinary operations, and integrate Vetter with payment processors and lab diagnostic platforms.
use_cases: Scheduling appointments from veterinary practice agents
Managing electronic medical records from clinical agents
Sending client reminders from communication agents
Processing invoices from revenue cycle agents
not_for: Large corporate veterinary hospitals requiring enterprise PIMS
Emergency or specialty referral practices with complex workflow
Mixed animal or farm animal practices without small animal focus
best_when: A small, mobile, or house call veterinary practice wants AI agents to automate appointment scheduling, medical records, client communication, and invoicing.
avoid_when: DEA CONTROLLED SUBSTANCE LOGGING: Automated prescription and dispensing management in veterinary software must maintain DEA Schedule II-V controlled substance records per DEA 21 CFR 1304; automated dispensing without manual DEA log verification creates federal compliance risk. VCPR for telemedicine — automated prescription generation via telemedicine integration requires established Veterinarian-Client-Patient Relationship (VCPR) per AVMA guidelines and state veterinary practice acts; telemedicine prescriptions without VCPR create state licensing board violations. Small vendor stability — Vetter is a small, independent veterinary software company; API investment and long-term platform stability should be evaluated before mission-critical integration.

auth.notes: Vetter uses API key authentication. REST API with JSON. Tel Aviv, Israel HQ. Founded 2012. Private, bootstrapped. Small and mobile veterinary practice focus. Cloud-native PIMS. Competes with Shepherd, eVetPractice, and ImproMed for cloud veterinary software.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier for solo practitioners; limited records and features
pricing.notes: Tel Aviv, Israel. Founded 2012. Bootstrapped. Small/mobile vet practice focus. Cloud PIMS. Competes with Shepherd and eVetPractice for cloud vet software.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; limited documentation

known_agent_gotchas: DEA CONTROLLED SUBSTANCE RISK: Automated prescription management must maintain DEA Schedule II-V drug logs; Vetter automated dispensing requires manual DEA log verification to comply with 21 CFR 1304 controlled substance regulations
VCPR telemedicine compliance — automated prescriptions via telemedicine require established VCPR; telemedicine prescriptions without VCPR compliance violate state veterinary practice acts
Small vendor API stability — Vetter is a small bootstrapped company; API documentation may be limited and API changes may occur without advance notice; implement version pinning and change monitoring
No webhooks — appointment and medical record events require polling; no native push for appointment confirmations or medical record updates
API key only — single API key authentication without OAuth or scopes; compromised key provides full practice data access
International data residency (Israel HQ) — Vetter operates from Israel; verify data processing agreements and data residency options for US practice data
agent_readiness.security_notes: Veterinary PIMS. SOC2. API key only. US/EU/IL. Patient records and prescription data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Veterinary PIMS. SOC2. API key. US/EU/IL. Patient and prescription data.
